description: Wallis Opposes Bill Closing U S to Aliens Ellis Island Chief Tells Senate Committee the Country Needs Immi grants Sees No Menace Is Foe of Literaey Test Favors Weeding Ont Process Abroad and the Proper Alloeation on Arrivals Froin The Tribunss Washlnoton Buream WASHINGTON Jan rKeforo the Senate Committee on Immigration to day Frederick A Wallis Commissioner of Immigration at New York opposed the Johnson bill which would shut out immigration for the period of a year Mr Wallis declared he believed this country needed immigration and that immigration should continue He op posed prohibiting immigration for six months and helr1 the problem of per manent legislation could be adjusted without attempting to cut oT immi grants provided the present legislation and system of viseing passports is kept in force Tho Commissioner made it clear he seevs no menace conerning which so much has been heard His statements commanded close attention on the part of the committee and are expected to have weight both with respect to ihe committees course nnd that of the Senate For Bigger Force Here Testimony of Mr Waliis showed he believes in a more effective system of examining and weediftg out immigrants before they sail
